topic: Claude Code Plan Mode Guide: How to Use Plan Mode Effectively in 2026

competitors.title: Claude Code Plan Mode Guide — A Complete Walkthrough
key_points: Plan Mode blocks write tools (Edit, MultiEdit, Write, Bash); allows Read, LS, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch, TodoRead/Write
3 activation methods: Shift+Tab, /plan slash command, prompt directive (type 'plan this task')
3-mode cycle: Normal → Plan → Auto-accept (Shift+Tab cycles through all three)
Recommended workflow: plan → review → auto-accept → verify
5 key use cases: framework migrations, security audits, multi-file features, dependency upgrades, unfamiliar codebases
Dual-Claude review pattern: use one session to plan, another to review
/effort max pairing for thorough analysis in plan mode
Known failure mode: Claude sometimes exits plan mode mid-session, requiring manual re-activation

competitors.title: Claude Code Plan Mode — Read-Only Research and Analysis Phase
key_points: Plan Mode creates a read-only state for research and analysis before coding begins
How it compares to 'just asking Claude to think first' — plan mode is enforced, not advisory
Best for complex, multi-step tasks where you want to review the approach before execution
Plan mode prevents accidental file modifications during exploratory analysis
Useful for codebase exploration, architecture decisions, and risk assessment

competitors.title: Claude Code Plan Mode: A Complete Guide to Safer AI Coding
key_points: Plan Mode = read-only exploration state; AI reads files/searches/codebase but cannot write/edit/execute
3 activation methods: Shift+Tab twice, /plan command, Auto Plan Mode setting ('maximum safety')
Plan → Execute workflow: Read → Map → Propose → Review → Approve? → Edit → Apply → Verify → Report
3 benefits: catch mistakes before they happen, stay in control, AI produces better work when constrained to think first
When to use: multi-file changes, production codebases, unfamiliar codebases, architectural decisions, unclear requirements, code review prep
When to skip: typo fixes, single-line changes, well-defined isolated tasks, exploration/questions
Auto Plan Mode setting available for teams that want maximum safety guardrails

competitors.title: Claude Code Best Practices: Context Engineering for 2026
key_points: CLAUDE.md is no longer optional — treat it as infrastructure like .gitignore
Martin Fowler's 'context engineering' concept: crafting the right context for AI tools
66% report AI solutions are 'almost right' (the 80% problem); 45% say debugging AI code is slower
Claude Code uses 5.5x fewer tokens than Cursor for equivalent tasks
Golden rule for CLAUDE.md: for each line, ask 'Would removing this cause Claude to make mistakes?' If not, cut it
Plan Mode separates research from implementation, preventing Claude from solving the wrong problem
The Two-Correction Rule: if you've corrected Claude more than twice on the same issue, /clear and start fresh
Subagents run in separate context windows, saving 40%+ of input tokens for codebase research
Batch reviews avoid decision fatigue — let Claude work through a series of changes, then review the batch with git diff
